meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Mastering Analytics: Solve Slow Development for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome slow development speed with expert analytics setup. Learn to optimize your workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code in this student guide.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from building features users don't need. Integrate robust event-tracking analytics immediately. By mapping user flows to hard data, you stop guessing and start optimizing. Use Antigravity to instrument your codebase and Claude Code to automate the deployment of tracking hooks, cutting your feedback loops by 80%.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a half-finished repository, the clock is ticking, and the feature you spent three days building feels like a ghost town. In the world of SaaS, 'slow development' isn't just about code complexity; it’s the agonizing silence of not knowing what your users are actually doing inside your application. You are building in the dark, and it is killing your momentum.

But the veil is about to lift. By integrating high-fidelity analytics, you transform your build process from a guessing game into a surgical operation. This guide will show you how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to instrument your app in minutes, allowing you to ship with the confidence of a senior engineer and the speed of a startup founder.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that define user success. This ensures you track value, not noise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase structure and suggest the top 3 high-value user interaction events I should track for a SaaS onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my current routes and suggest event names for PostHog or Mixpanel integration.'

steps.title: Automate Telemetry Boilerplate
steps.description: Don't manually wire every click. Use AI to generate a standardized event-tracking wrapper across your components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable 'useAnalytics' hook that wraps tracking events and handles user identity resolution automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a TypeScript analytics provider and a custom hook for event tracking.'

steps.title: Instrument Critical User Paths
steps.description: Focus on the 'Happy Path'. If you can't track it, it doesn't exist in your production roadmap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan my login and signup components. Refactor them to inject an analytics trigger on every successful submission.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add tracking events to all buttons in the src/components/auth directory.'

steps.title: Validate Data Flow with Claude
steps.description: Ensure your events are firing correctly before you push to production, using automated simulation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that simulates a user journey and verifies that all analytics events are correctly dispatched.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Verify analytics event triggers in the test suite for the main dashboard.'

steps.title: Looping Feedback into Development
steps.description: Use the data from your new analytics dashboard to prioritize your next feature set, eliminating wasted effort.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on a hypothetical 20% drop-off rate at the dashboard, suggest three UI improvements to increase engagement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'How can I optimize the dashboard component based on high bounce rate analytics?'

pro_tips: Track 'failed' events as aggressively as 'success' events; failures tell you exactly where your UX is broken.
Use distinct user IDs from day one, even for anonymous visitors, to stitch sessions together once they sign up.
Avoid 'event fatigue' by tracking only what you have a plan to act upon; too much data is as paralyzing as no data.
Implement server-side tracking for critical financial or authentication events to prevent ad-blockers from skewing your metrics.
Treat your analytics schema like your database schema: version it and keep it documented in your repo.
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, spend 48 hours observing your users' behavior in your analytics dashboard. Use these insights to ship one high-impact fix rather than three low-impact features.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student writing code; you are an AI Builder making data-driven decisions. This is the difference between a project that stays in your portfolio and one that gets real users. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
